======================================================================
PAGE: Pattern
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Pattern
======================================================================

Pattern
Pattern
Artwork by
Isaac Stewart
Biographical information
Race
Spren
Ethnicity
Cultivation
Nationality
Shadesmar
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Abilities
Nahel bonding
, affording
Surgebinding
Social Information
Title(s)
Cryptic
,
Liespren
Occupation
Lightweaverspren
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Pattern
is the name
Shallan
gives to the Cryptic spren with whom she bonds. He says his real name is a series of numbers and agrees to Pattern as being sufficient enough a name.
Pattern's bond with Shallan grants her the Surges of Illumination and Transformation.
Appearance
Artwork from
Shallan's sketchbook
Drawing by
Ben McSweeney
Shallan initially describes Pattern as " ... a sequence of complex lines with sharp angles and arrowhead shapes." She then describes him to
Jasnah
as a pattern of sharp lines, like a maze, impossibly formed on the surface of a wave in the water in which they were sailing on the
Wind's Pleasure
.
“
Raised, like an embossing, it had a complex pattern with a haunting symmetry. Its tiny lines twisted and turned through its mass, somehow lifting the surface of the wood, like iron scrollwork under a taut tablecloth.
”
Pattern appears as a complex three-dimensional scribble capable of sliding and floating around, occasionally lying flat in a two-dimensional pattern, often adhering to Shallan's dress. He can take the form of an illusion that Shallan creates, which is a venture he seems to enjoy when given the chance.
According to
Adolin
, Pattern is a tall figure in too-stiff robes with a changing symbol for a head. Further, he feels he can tell Pattern from other Cryptics because there is a spring to Pattern’s step; he bounces when certain other Cryptics glide.
Personality
Pattern
Fan art by exmakina
[1]
Pattern's personality progression is similar to that of
Sylphrena
, whence he goes from a primitive, non-communicative state to being able to put together more and more complex thoughts to being able to understand abstract communication.
As he does so, Pattern seems to be a scholarly and excitable spren, exulting in finding truths, lies, and understanding new concepts. Upon discovering sarcasm and humor, he seemed to latch on to those two styles of communications quickly, soon mimicking Shallan's sometimes inappropriate wit.
With regard to his sarcasm, Pattern states ...
“
"Insults in particular will be of great use to my people, as they are truths and lies combined in a quite interesting manner."
”
Still ...
“
"I know ... little of humans,"
Pattern said.
"They break. Their minds break. You did not break. Only cracked."
She continued her washing.
"It is the lies that save you,"
Pattern said.
"The lies that drew me."
”
In stating as much, Pattern acknowledged that he was obviously drawn to Shallan for very specific reasons.
Characteristics
Pattern believes that humans do not understand patterns. He does not understand sleep, nor does he truly understand scholarship.
“
"I find sleeping very odd,"
Pattern said.
"I know that all beings in the Physical Realm engage in it. Do you find it pleasant? You fear nonexistence, but is not unconsciousness the same thing?"
"With sleep, it's only temporary."
"Ah. It is all right, because in the morning, you each return to sentience."
"Well, that depends on the person,"
Shallan said absently.
"For many of them, 'sentience' might be too generous a term ... ."
Pattern hummed, trying to sort through to the meaning of what she said. Finally, he buzzed an approximation of a laugh.
”
Pattern wishes he could see a play. A "group lie," as he calls it. He thinks he could understand people through the lies they want to be told.
Biography
Pattern
Fan art by jariv
[2]
Shallan first consciously remembers seeing Pattern on her sketchpad. He rises from the page like an embossing. When Shallan went to feel the ridges on the page, Pattern moved, frightening her. Next, she saw him in the water, formed on the surface of a wave.
Later, she saw him on the cabin wall beside her bunk on the
Wind's Pleasure
,
acting like a newly bonded spren. The simple answer was the true one. He had been newly bonded.
Since then, she's seen him from the corner of her eye, appearing in the grain of wood, the cloth on the back of a sailor's shirt. Each time, when she looked right at him, he vanished. Jasnah indicated to her that 'it' was likely harmless.
Shallan soon realizes that Pattern is one of the "symbolheads" she'd seen before. Looking directly at him, he begins to fade, but she takes a
Memory
of him, then applies her charcoal pencil to paper, yet quickly switches to pen and ink for crisper, sharper lines.
Still, Pattern's complex repetitions twist down into infinity. She realizes that a pen could never capture this 'thing' perfectly. She draws him spiraling out of a center point, then re-creates each branch off the center, which has its own swirl of tiny lines.
When she finishes, the pattern
rises
before her and she hears a distinct sigh from the paper, as if in relief. Her drawn pattern then leaves the paper and moves onto the floor. Then, 'it' came to the leg of her cot, wrapped around it, and climbed upward and onto her blanket. He was an exact form beneath the blanket.
To Shallan, 'it' didn't look dangerous, but she trembled nonetheless. This pattern was different from the symbolheads in her drawings, but it was also the
same
somehow. To her, this was a flattened-out version, without torso or limbs.
“
It was an abstraction of one of them, just as a circle with a few lines in it could represent a human's face on the page.
”
Shadesmar
When Shallan sees Pattern in Shadesmar, he is in the form of one of the symbolhead figures (i.e., a Cryptic) that she had seen in earlier visions. This would appear to be Pattern's 'true' form. He has a robe that looks as if it was chiseled from stone and an ever-changing 3D pattern in place of a head.
[citation needed]
Kholinar
In
Kholinar
, when Shallan asks Pattern if he knows anything about
Wit
, Pattern tells her no, but that he feels like one of them.
Later,
Veil
tells Pattern to hang on the wall of a hallway next to
Kharat
.
Pattern softly hums a reply: Indulge. Get something to drink. Join the
revel
.
History
Pattern has been with Shallan since she was a young girl.
According to
Brandon
, the bonding process with Pattern started when Shallan was young but she then pushed him away. He withdrew mostly to the
Cognitive Realm
until the bonding was resumed, and she pulled him fully into the Physical Realm when she drew him.
Though he was not the
Shardblade
she used to kill her
mother
when her mother tried to kill her,
*
Pattern was present in the aftermath, perceived by Shallan to be glowing from the cracks within her
father
's strongbox.
**
She also perceives Pattern as a monster during this time.
Pattern is attracted to lies. It is one reason he's attracted to Shallan; her 'delicious' lies. But he also knows that for Shallan to become the person she is meant to be - with him, that she must accept all of who she now is ... and was.
“
"Shallan,"
Pattern said,
moving closer to her. "I know that you have forgotten much of what once was. Those lies attracted me. But you cannot continue like this; you must admit the truth about me. About what I can do, and what we have done. Mmm ... More, you must know yourself. And remember."
...
"You wish to help,"
Pattern said.
"You wish to prepare for the Everstorm, the spren of the unnatural one. You must become something. I did not come to you merely to teach you tricks of light."
"You came to learn,"
Shallan said, staring at her map.
"That’s what you said."
"I came to learn. We became to do something greater."
"Would you have me unable to laugh?"
she demanded, suddenly holding back tears.
"Would you have me crippled? That is what those memories would do to me. I can be what I am because I cut them off."
...
"Mmmm ... Such a deep lie,"
Pattern whispered.
"A deep lie indeed. But still, you must obtain your abilities. Learn again if you have to."
”
Pattern says that he has always existed. Further, that spren are not 'born' as men are, and cannot truly die as men do. He says that patterns are eternal, as is fire, as is the wind, are all spren. Still, he says that he was not in this state when the Knights Radiant existed. That he was not aware. Even so, he says that he was ... everything. In everything. Yet, he cannot fully explain it because language is insufficient; he would need numbers.
Further, he tells Shallan that she will eventually betray her
oaths
, thereby breaking his mind, leaving him dead. But, he says, the opportunity is worth the cost. That his kind are too static. That they always change, but only in the same way, over and over.
Additionally, he tells her that in coming to this world of hers, he had to give up many things. That the transition was traumatic. He explains that his memory returns slowly, but that he is pleased at the chance.Missing parameters
Pattern's insistence that Shallan reclaim herself and her abilities finally hits home. Shallan eventually does reclaim her past and, in so doing, realizes that by summoning her
living
Shardblade and ramming it into the slot of the
Oathgate
's operating mechanism, she is able to discern the procedure for making the Oathgate 'work'.
“
He formed in her hands, the Blade she'd used to kill. The hidden soul.
”
Pattern knows that in order to fully be the Lightweaver she is meant to be, Shallan must have spoken the First Ideal of the
Knights Radiant
(which she did, several years ago), then speak only truths.
“
"Lightweavers make no oaths beyond the first ... . You must speak truths."
”
Being that Shallan is somewhat vexed by that which she accepted of her past, Pattern tells her that he understands why she hates him; that he didn't mean to help help her kill her mother, but that it is what he did.
“
"It's all right,"
Shallan said in a hushed voice as the wind died down.
"Just ... just let me draw."
"Mmm ... "
Pattern said.
"A powerful lie ... "
”
Abilities
Pattern has the ability to read ancient languages - such as
Dawnchant
- as they are written in a "pattern" according to him.
He can also unlock locks by growing very small and moving into keyholes. Shallan had had him try on locks in her rooms, and he'd been able to unlock those as he had that of Tyn's trunk.
He also has the ability to mimic voices perfectly, which he uses to save Shallan from being killed by
Tyn
.
However, he is unable to hide his presence completely or to disappear completely like other spren, despite that his presence is limited in the Physical Realm. This probably underlies why he doesn't retain
Stormlight
well.
(Stormlight is meant to be contained by appropriate physical objects or entities.)
Pattern isn't like dead-spren Shardblades; he can be summoned more quickly than each their requisite
ten
heartbeats.
Notes
*
That Shardblade was
Testament
, Shallan's first Cryptic.
**
Pattern might not have been alone in that strongbox.
Possibly the best ever representation of Pattern is
here
.

PAGE: Phendorana
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Phendorana
======================================================================

Phendorana
Biographical information
Race
Spren
Ethnicity
Honor
Gender
Female
Status
Dead
Abilities
Nahel bonding
, affording
Surgebinding
Social Information
Title(s)
Honorspren
Occupation
Windrunnerspren
Appears in
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Phendorana
was an honorspren with a level of significance and/or command amongst those of her kind.
She appeared to
Rock
on a plateau of the
Shattered Plains
along with two dozen or more of her kind. They glowed a faint blue, and clustered around her, swirling about in the air, their focus the practicing
bridgemen
- and would-be
squires
- learning to draw
Stormlight
into themselves.
After Rock made the
Unkalaki
signs of respect toward the spren woman,
Syl
told Rock that he is so wonderfully respectful, and that it borders on being silly.
When he asked Syl whether they - collectively - are her kin, and if the woman at their front was her
nuatoma
, she told him that they kind of are, and that Phendorana was ... maybe.
Syl told Rock that they wouldn't speak to her. She thought that they assumed that if they did, they'd have to admit to her that they were wrong about her with regard to her search for
Kaladin
. And, that they absolutely hated being wrong.
Phendorana studied Rock, then rested her hand over one of the bowls containing
spheres
that he'd arranged for her, and drew out the Stormlight. Afterward, she left, turning into a streak of light and zipping away.
The other spren remained, a mottled collection of clouds, ribbons, people, bunches of leaves, and other natural objects. They flitted overhead, watching the practicing men and women.
Appearance
Phendorana appears significantly larger than Syl in the
Physical Realm
.
Wearing a dignified gown, she appears as a tall, human-sized woman with long hair streaming behind her.
She has mature features and wears no-nonsense Thaylen-style clothing.
Personality
Syl vaguely remembers a voice, and that the spren woman reprimanded her when she was looking for Kaladin.
History
Phendorana was a leader amongst her kind at the time when Syl was searching for and then eventually bonding with Kaladin.
She later bonded with
Teft
.
Teft stood stunned as
Moash
pulled a strange dagger from his belt and slammed it down - right where Phendorana was kneeling. She looked up with surprise and took the knife straight in the forehead. Then she screamed. Teft leaped for her, howling, watching in horror as she shrank, writhing as Moash's dagger pinned her to the floor. Her essence burned, flaring outward like an explosion.
Something ripped inside Teft. Something deeper than his own heart. A part of his soul, his being, was torn away. He collapsed immediately, falling near the white spot in the sand that was all that remained of Phendorana.

crew in

PAGE: Punio
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Punio
======================================================================

Punio
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Herdazian
Nationality
Alethi
Gender
Male
Status

Family

(cousin),
Chilinko
(uncle?), Lopen's
Mother
(aunt?),
Huio
(cousin?),

(cousin?)

Appears in
Words of Radiance
Punio
is one of
Lopen
's cousins who appears to join
Bridge Four
. He is the first of said cousins to be noticed by
Kaladin
.
Originally a member of
Roion
's army, he swore to Kaladin for his cousin, Lopen. He'd heard Lopen's mother say that Lopen's cohort needed more guards and so came to help. Lopen got him a uniform amongst other things.
Appearance
He is a bald, muscular man.

PAGE: Puuli
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Puuli
======================================================================

Puuli
Puuli
Fan art by Botanicaxu
[1]
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Natan
Nationality
Natan
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Family
Grandfather
,
Sakin
(wife)
Occupation
Lighthouse Keeper
Appears in
Oathbringer
Puuli
is the lighthouse keeper at a little port on the crumbling eastern edge of
Roshar
, living in a fine stone hut.
His name for the lighthouse is
Defiance
.
Appearance
He is squat and short, with blue skin.
Characteristics
He is powerful and enduring.
Beliefs
Puuli believes that
Kelek
himself had broken apart the land in the middle of a storm, making a new prime spot for homes. A place particularly for the wealthy.
He also believes that, with regard to his grandfather's
prediction
, this was now, with a new storm. Darkest nights. A tragedy. And a sign.
Biography
Puuli drags a cart through the port town, scavenging debris left behind in the wake of the
Everstorm
.
He tells Sakin that the new storm was truly tragic, even as he is secretly happy about it. The storm had brought him so much wood, scraps of
rockbuds
, and branches from trees.
Shortly thereafter, he tells
Au-lam
the same thing while helping him clear the refuse of his barn, much of which winds up in his own cart.
He then tells
Hema-dak
the same thing, just before agreeing to watch her children so she could run a broth to her sister, who was sick with the fever.
Then, he also tells the
Drummer
brothers the same thing, just before helping them by pulling a tattered sail from the surf and stretching it out on the rocks.
Puuli thinks that his family was a lot like the lighthouse themselves. Squat, short, but powerful. And enduring.

PAGE: Raboniel
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Raboniel
======================================================================

Raboniel
Raboniel
Fan art by conjchamberlain.
[1]
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Parsh
Gender
Female
Status
Dead
Family
Daughter
Occupation
Fused
Appears in
Rhythm of War
Raboniel
was the Fused that led the invasion of
Urithiru
because she believed the
Sibling
was a
deadeye
. She is called the "Lady of Wishes" by the
Nine
,
but the Sibling calls her "The Lady of Pains" as she was originally known thousands of years ago.
Raboniel forces
Navani
to refer to her as the "Ancient One."
Appearance
She was a tall
femalen
, of the
fannahn-im
- the builders who had created the
palace
- with a tall topknot of hair and carapace like a helmet otherwise covering her head. She wore a luxurious robe and was willowy, with a narrow figure and long arms, fingers fully twice the length of those of
Venli
.
Personality
She was an extremely intelligent scholar, fascinated by
Radiants
, their
oaths
,
Surges
, and
spren
.
She was looking for ways to combine
Voidlight
and
Stormlight
.
Character
Leshwi
thought her mad after the last
Desolation
, and Raboniel was to remain asleep. Raboniel told Leshwi that she discovered the technology for the suppression of
fabrials
thousands of years ago in the tower of Urithiru.
“
"Rayse is the unknowable evil, I wanted the knowable antagonist. Not even necessarily evil, evil-ish in Raboniel."
”
–
Brandon Sanderson
Characteristics
Leshwi explains to Venli that Raboniel developed a disease intended to kill all humans on Roshar, but which would also likely kill many
singers
. It didn't work.
“
"During the last Return, she developed a disease intended to kill all humans on the planet. Near the end, it was discovered that the disease would likely kill many singers as well. She released it anyway … only to find, to all of our fortunes, that it did not work as expected. Fewer than one in ten humans were killed, and one in a hundred singers."
”
–Leshwi to Venli
Raboniel was very biased in her thinking, particularly when it came to the
Surge
of Adhesion on Roshar.
Beliefs
Invasion
Fan art by
Petar Penev
Raboniel believed that humans had been allowed too much time to grow. That they ran rampant across Roshar. That they had steel weapons and advanced military tactics. That they outstripped her own people's knowledge in areas.
Further, that the one thing humans didn't yet have was mastery over their powers. That there were few among them of the
Fourth Ideal
- perhaps only one individual - and that they didn't have full access to the tower of Urithiru, now that the Sibling was (according to her) dead.
She believed that her people must have striked and seized the tower from the humans.
Intent
She'll lead a team into the tower of Urithiru, then secure control of the Sibling's heart. Using her natural talents, and the gifts of
Odium
, she'll corrupt that heart, and turn the tower to her needs. The humans would fall; their powers would not work, but hers and those of her cohorts would.
She forces Navani to do expereiments with Light and helps her, which results in their common writing of
Rhythm of War
.
Family
Raboniel
Fan art by Caio Santos
[2]
She has an insane Fused daughter.
Death
Hers was an
anti-Investiture
death.
That said, Brandon has indicated that readers can see her in
Herald
(forthcoming) flashbacks.
Speculation
From what Raboniel has said, it seems that
Threnodite
silver is special. She describes a chain made of it as a "priceless object,"
and if it was indeed that which
Mraize
gave her, she was so shocked when she saw it that her voice was devoid of
Rhythms
when she spoke.
She describes it as "a chain from the lands of the dead,"
which, combined with the super expensive Threnodite silver chain readers saw in
Celebrant
in Oathbringer,
makes one think that this would also be a Threnodite silver chain.

======================================================================
PAGE: Radiant
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Radiant
======================================================================

Radiant
Radiant
Artwork by Petar Penev.
[1]
Biographical information
Race
Human
Ethnicity
Veden
Nationality
Jah Kevedian
/
Alethi
Gender
Female
Status
Alive
Abilities
Surgebinding
(
Lightweaver
)
,
Shardbearer
Social Information
Aliases
Shallan Davar
,
Veil
,
Swiftspren
,
Kishi
,
Formless
Title(s)
Brightness
,
Little Knife
Family
House Davar
,
House Kholin
Occupation
Ghostblood
(affiliated),
Knight Radiant
Appears in
Oathbringer
Radiant
is an alternative identity whom Shallan Lightweaves on herself in order to better learn how to wield her
Shardblade
while sparring with
Adolin
.
Before she approaches the
Cult of Moments
, Shallan/Veil/Radiant thinks that she's just another
spren
; emotion made carnal.
At the end of the
Battle of Thaylen Field
,
Jasnah
attempts to grab Shallan by the wrist. After two failed attempts, it is discovered that Radiant is the real Shallan.
Background
Radiant was first created after Shallan regained the memories of killing her
mother
with her Shardblade, and thus became unable to wield the weapon due to the trauma surrounding it.
Shallan would often retreat into Radiant whenever she and Adolin practiced sword fighting, or when she would have to summon her Shardblade.
Personality
Radiant is Shallan's idealized version of a Radiant Knight. She views herself as both a warrior and a scholar, and enjoys both sword fighting and learning.
Radiant is often blunt and prefers to approach matters directly.  Unlike Shallan and Veil, she is unskilled in espionage.
Radiant tends to be rather humorless, avoids small talk, and speaks in a formal manner even when addressing friends.  Veil compares Radiant's demeanor to an
honorspren
, and in jest, Shallan remarks that: "Radiant is as lighthearted as a chasmfiend, one with a particularly large emerald inside."
Radiant often encourages Shallan to tell Adolin about her involvement with the Ghostbloods.
Radiant wants to practice with the sword and spend time around Adolin.
Characteristics
Radiant's clothing of choice is a
vakama
, which is a traditional Veden warrior's clothing and is similar to the Alethi
takama
. The clothing is blue embroidered over red with gold woven between, matching the colors of her home kingdom of
Jah Keved
.  She often gets strange looks from the Alethi when she wears this clothing due to wearing such colors while being married to an Alethi
highprince
, and because a vakama is traditionally considered a man's outfit.
When Radiant first manifested, she had the same hair color as Shallan. However, she later changed her hair color to blond so people could externally tell when she was in control.
Radiant has a habit of clasping her hands behind her back, and tends to maintain an upright posture.
Like Veil, Radiant sees herself as older than Shallan.
Relationships
Adolin
Unlike Veil, Radiant likes him. Shallan thinks that Radiant could be his perfect bride, but this thought makes her feel cold inside.
Radiant voted in favor of Shallan marrying Adolin.
Shallan
Radiant fully accepts her role as a protector to Shallan, and tries in anyway she can to ease Shallan's burdens.  She feels it is her duty to protect Shallan from physical dangers, but also encourages Shallan to remain strong when she's feeling scared.
Shallan tends to idolize Radiant, and sees her as incapable of wrongdoing; this causes Radiant to feel guilty about lying that she killed Ialai.
Shallan likes to tease Radiant about her lack of humor.
Veil
Radiant and Veil often don't see eye-to-eye in their methods for solving problems; and even when they do agree, they still sometimes argue.
Radiant doesn't enjoy espionage and would rather simply ask questions, though she does acknowledge that Veil's methods can get results.
Despite their differences, Veil and Radiant both strive for efficiency.
Ultimately, Radiant acknowledges that despite the difference in their methods, they both exist to protect and help Shallan.  Radiant accepts that Veil can help Shallan in ways she cannot.
Radiant despises Veils excessive drinking, and condemns Veil whenever she gives the three of them a hangover.
Notes
During an earlier part of the referenced chapter, Radiant was described as wearing
Shardplate
.
Speculation
It has been interpreted by numerous readers that due to her reaction when Jasnah asks Shallan just where her armor is,
[citation needed]
and
Brandon
having said that Shallan is farther along in her
Ideals
than
Kaladin
is, that she's able to summon Shardplate. Shallan being Shallan, she hasn't wanted to do so.
References

PAGE: Rayse
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rayse
======================================================================

Rayse
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Unknown
Nationality
Unknown
Gender
Male
Status
Dead
Abilities
Shardholder
(previously),
Surgebinding
(assumed)
Appears in
The Way of Kings
(referenced),
Words of Radiance
(referenced),
Oathbringer
(referenced),
Rhythm of War
Rayse
was the Shardholder of the
Shard
of
Adonalsium
,
Odium
,
who was - for a time - one of the three Shards located on
Roshar
.
At some point in the past, Rayse made a pact with Cephandrius (i.e.,
Hoid
) which (presumptuously) prohibited the god from inflicting upon Cephandrius any type of pain.
This oath bound not only the
Vessel
, but the Shard itself.
[citation needed]
Rayse benefitted from Odium's intent
inasmuch as Odium was described as loathsome, crafty, and dangerous.
Rayse
wanted
to become what he was. He downed three Shards and survived by a combination of skill, ability, numbers, and un-dispersed power.
Character
According to
Wit
(i.e., Hoid), Rayse was arrogant and enjoyed being worshipped. He never did anything without delighting in how he could show off. He was also careful. Subtle.
Characteristics
According to
Brandon
, Rayse is VERY scared of
Sazed
. However, given that Sazed is a composite of two diametrically opposed Shards, he finds it very difficult to act.
History
Some of the few Shards that Rayse
Splintered
were Ambition, Devotion and Dominion, though this was accomplished long ago.
He went after Ambition first, but didn't find Ambition until after going after Devotion and Dominion. That said, Ambition was number one on his hit list.
Rayse was afraid that Ambition that would rival him, so he decided to take it down. But then he got trapped in the Rosharan
system
.
Quotes
“
Ati was once a kind and generous man, and you saw what became of him. Rayse, on the other hand, was among the most loathsome, crafty, and dangerous individuals I had ever met.
”
–Hoid to
Frost
“
My path has been chosen very deliberately. Yes, I agree with everything you have said about Rayse, including the severe danger he presents.
”
–Frost to Hoid
“
Rayse is captive. He cannot leave the system he now inhabits. His destructive potential is, therefore, inhibited.
”
–Frost to Hoid
“
Whether this was Tanavast's design or not, millennia have passed without Rayse taking the life of another of the sixteen.
*
”
–Frost to Hoid
Relationships
Before the Shattering, Rayse had personal relationships with some of the other Vessels. Although he knew them all (they were all in the same place), he didn't necessarily interact with them.
Notes
*
Tanavast
was the Shardholder of
Honor
. He also constructed the confrontation with Rayse in such a way that bound Odium to
Braize
.
Rayse was killed by
Taravangian
with
Nightblood
soon after the reconquest of
Urithiru
.
According to Brandon, Rayse needed to go because he'd been essentially defeated at the end of Oathbringer, when Dalinar didn't go over to him.
All of his rage, and everything he was trying to do couldn't make that happen. He was defeated, at least in a philosophical sense.
“
"Now you can bring a defeated enemy back to be a threat again. You can find a new way to make them a threat, but I knew - in this book - Kaladin was not going to fall to him either. But once you've had two books in a row with the characters machinations not - things stymied by the heroes. I needed a different villain at that point."
”
–Brandon Sanderson
Q&A with Brandon
Q. Rayse (i.e., Odium) was very methodical with the order in which he went after other Shards. Hypothetically if he'd visited the Rosharan system all those years ago but managed to avoid being trapped and was able to continue his mission right away, which of the known Shards would've been next up on Rayse's hit list?
A. He expected Ruin to implode. So he might have gone for Autonomy, double crossing them.

PAGE: Re-Shephir
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Re-Shephir
======================================================================

Re-Shephir
Biographical information
Race
Unmade
Gender
Female
Status
Alive
Social Information
Aliases
Midnight Mother
Appears in
The Way of Kings
(mentioned),
Oathbringer
Re-Shephir
is one of the Unmade, a mysterious group of
odious
spren
on
Roshar
. Also referred to as the Midnight Mother, she appears in the depths of
Urithiru
where she is discovered first by
Renarin
, then by
Adolin
, and lastly by
Shallan
, who eventually interacts with her.
Appearance
Shallan sees a dark mass wriggling deep inside slits within the tower of Urithiru, squeezing between walls like goo, but with bits jutting out. These bits appear as elbows, ribs, fingers splayed along a wall, each knuckle bending backward.
The thing twists, head deforming in the tiny confines, and looks toward her. Shallan sees eyes reflecting her
Stormlight
, twin spheres set in a mashed head, a distorted human visage.
Later identified as Re-Shephir, she appears as a large mass that reflects the light, like glistening tar. She is an undulating shape, a living darkness that seeps, though not swiftly.
Characterisics
Midnight Mother meets Shallan
Fan art by Petar Penev
[1]
She casts a dark presence throughout the towering city and, when confronted, births
Midnight Essence
with her own dark essence.
There is an inevitability in the way she coats everything, flowing up the sides of walls, coating a ceiling. On the ground, shapes split from her main mass, becoming figures that step as if from surf. Creatures that have two feet and soon grow faces, with clothing that ripples into existence.
Shallan recognizes that these creatures look like themselves; she and her companions, some of whom were originally members of
Bridge Four
(i.e.,
Teft
,
Lopen
, and
Drehy
, amongst other members).
History
Re-Shephir brutally murdered
Vedekar Perel
in the tower at
Urithiru
, in the same manner that
Adolin
had killed
Sadeas
.
Impersonating
Veil
in a long coat and a hat, Re-Shepir also stabbed
Rock
in the same manner that Shallan (as Veil) had stabbed a man, because Rock looked similar to that man.
Epigraphs
“
"Re-Shephir, the Midnight Mother, giving birth to abominations with her essence so dark, so terrible, so consuming. She is here! She watches me die!"
”
–Dated Shashabev, 1173, 8 seconds pre-death. Subject: a darkeyed dock-worker in his forties, father of three.
“
Re-Shephir, the Midnight Mother, is another Unmade who appears to have been destroyed at Aharietiam.
”
–From
Hessi
's
Mythica
, page 250
“
The Midnight Mother created monsters of shadow and oil, dark imitations of creatures she saw consumed. Their description matches no spren I can find in modern literature.
”
–From Hessi's
Mythica
, page 252
Notes
Brandon
has confirmed that the Unmade are
Splinters
of Odium.
Odium splintered himself and created the Unmade to be his influence on Roshar. It is through the Unmade that Odium may be able to influence people in how they think or act.
References

PAGE: Red
CATEGORIES: Characters

Red
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Alethi
Nationality
Alethi
Gender
Male
Status
Unknown
Abilities
Surgebinding
(
Lightweaving
)
Occupation
Soldier,
Member of the
Unseen Court
Appears in
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Red
is a darkeyed soldier who joined
Highprince
Vamah
's call to arms. He joined with five of his buddies, all of whom are now dead.
Red deserted the Alethi army, along with
Gaz
and others, because the
lighteyes
stopped fighting to win the war and started fighting for
spheres
instead.
His first encounter with
Shallan Davar
is as a deserter/mercenary whilst in pursuit of her caravan on it's way to the
Shattered Plains
,
Tvlakv
and his crew as her escort.
Red and his cohorts are then implored by Shallan to intervene in a slaughter before them, and are convinced by her that they can erase their pasts and start over as heroes.
At a drop point, Red deposited a pack for her that contained
Veil
's outfit.
Appearance
He is a lanky, bearded man.
Personality
Red, along with his fellow deserters, is an affable man who seized a chance to live a normal life again and is determined to hold to it. He maintained his discipline and was quick and eager to serve Shallan.

======================================================================
PAGE: Redin
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Redin
======================================================================

Redin
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Veden
Nationality
Jah Kevedian
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Physical Description
Eye Color
Intense blue/Dark brown
Family
Highprince
Valam
(father)
Appears in
Words of Radiance
Redin
is the bastard son of Highprince Valam of Jah Keved; his mother is an unknown darkeyed woman. This heritage caused him to be born dual-eyed, with one eye light and the other dark.
Appearance
Redin's face was long and slender, looking as if it had been pinched.
One of his eyes is described as "intense blue" and the other as "dark brown". He was both
lighteyed
and dark.
History
Four years prior to the major events within Words of Radiance, Redin is a newcomer to
Lin Davar
's table, sent there by his father. He confronts
Brightlord
Davar about the death of
Shallan
's
mother
, claiming that others tell a different story than the one Brightlord Davar is telling with regard to who killed his wife. Redin states that such rumors are troublesome and that his father - His Highness, Highprince Valam - wouldn't be able to ignore the action of a brightlord under his rule who was to have murdered a lighteyed woman of rank.
Shallan recognizes Redin as the bastard son of Valam, noting that the son's father used him as an executioner and assassin, or so it was said.
Within the one
interlude
in which father and son interact, Valam refers to Redin as 'Bastard' instead of by name. Still, Redin appears to show genuine concern for his father as the man is on his sickbed. At this point,
Taravangian
sees Redin's demeanor as contrasting starkly with his reputation as his father's pet assassin.
“
Taravangian inspected this Redin, curious. This was the king's ruthless executioner that he had read about? This concerned, helpless-looking man?
”
After his brief conversation with Taravangian, Valam instructs Redin to kill him with a knife through his heart. At first, Redin objects, but then does so with such force that it makes Taravangian jump. Redin then salutes his dead father and shoves his way out of the sickroom.
Mrall
recognized the father's disdain for his bastard son, despite all that the father had done to him.
“
"Cruel, what Valam did to that one,"
Mrall said, nodding toward the bastard, who shoved his way out of the stormwagon and into the light.
”
Quotes
“
"Highprince Valam might be dead, Brightness
Tyn
,"
the spanreed wrote
" ... His forces are gearing up to seize Vedenar, however, so if he's dead, his bastard son is likely pretending he is not.
”
“
The messenger gave
Father
a look of disgust, a demeaning sneer. It said, 'I am a bastard, but even I am not as low as you.'
”
Notes
“
One of his eyes was intense blue. The other dark brown. Both lighteyed and dark.
”
This description is so rare and is the apparent trait of a lighteye interbreeding with a darkeye. The condition is known as
heterochromia
.
[1]
Lighteyed
Graves
married a darkeyed woman and had a son with her who displays the same condition.

Relis Ruthar
Biographical information

Social Information
Title(s)
Brightlord
Family
Jayla Ruthar
(father),
Elit Ruthar
(cousin)
Occupation
Noble
Appears in
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
Relis Ruthar
is a full
Shardbearer
, owning both
Plate
and
Blade
, and one of few in his father's
warcamp
on the
Shattered Plains
. He is his father's star Shardbearer and the current dueling champion.
Appearance
He is described as " ... a man with a face like a shovel - flat and wide, with a nose that seemed like it had been smashed.
He is further described as wearing black armor, eventually trailing
Stormlight
in a crack in the vambrace on his left forearm.
Dueling
Relis, Elit's cousin, was approached by
Adolin
and offered a duel, the spoils of which would be Adolin's Plate and Blade,  plus
Renarin
's Plate and Blade, and the Shard Adolin had won from
Erraniv
; five Shards to Relis's two (i.e., Relis's Plate and Blade). Relis refused the duel, which Adolin then offered to Elit, who accepted. The spoils were to be Adolin's five Shards to Elit's one.
Since the dueling agreement between Relis and Adolin had been refused by Relis, yet agreed upon by Elit, the number of duelists Adolin would face had never been agreed upon. Upon entering the arena, Elit was accompanied by Relis,
Jakamav
, and
Abrobadar
. Elit was garbed in a set of the
king
's slate grey Plate, yet it was Relis to whom the dueling agreement had been attributed (since it was he whom Adolin had originally approached with the challenge), and Adolin hadn't specified that he'd face Relis and
only
one other.
Unfortunately, when Renarin joined his brother (supposedly in Adolin's defense), Relis went after Renarin instead. When
Kaladin
joined Adolin - in Renarin's defense - Relis lost his advantage, not thinking a simple spearman was a threat and, subsequently, he exited the duel and forfeited the match, losing his Shards with the decision.
Soldiering
In the fight against those
Parshendi
and
parshmen
- named
Voidbringers
- moving northward and having captured much of Alethkar, Relis tried to gather the remaining Alethi forces in the country, but was pushed back toward
Herdaz
, suffering at the hands of the
Fused
.

Restares
Biographical information

Restares
is an Alethi Brightlord and the presumed leader of the
Sons of Honor
, a group which seeks to bring the Heralds back to
Roshar
. He is working with
Amaram
and advised him to steal both the
Shardplate
and
Shardblade
that
Kaladin
had won in killing the
Shardbearer
,
Helaran
.
King
Gavilar
mentioned him as a potential suspect behind his assassination before
Szeth
revealed that the
Parshendi
had employed the
Assassin in White
instead.
Appearance
He is a short man with thinning hair.
History
Mraize
is aware of Restares, and that both the
Ghostbloods
and the Sons had been competing for information about
Urithiru
for some time.
Following the creation of the
Everstorm
, Restares receives a letter, written in glyphs, from
Amaram
:
“
I can only conclude ... that we have been successful, Restares. The reports from Dalinar's army indicate that Voidbringers were not only spotted, but fought. Red eyes, ancient powers. They have apparently unleashed a new storm upon this world.
... I do not exult in this success ... . Lives will be lost. It has ever been our burden as Sons of Honor. To return the Heralds, to return the domination of the Church, we had to put the world into a crisis.
That crisis we now have, a terrible one. The Heralds will return. How can they not, with the problems we now face? But many will die. So very many. Nalan send that it is worth the loss. Regardless, I will have more information soon. When I next write you, I hope to do so from Urithiru.
”
He is the man Mraize sends
Veil
to find - and, she suspects, the man he wanted her to kill - plus, he's one of the Heralds.

PAGE: Rii Oracle
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rii_Oracle
======================================================================

Rii Oracle
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Elantrian
Nationality
Elantrian
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Social Information
Aliases
Riino
Occupation
Fortune Teller
Appears in
Oathbringer
The
Rii Oracle
is an alter-ego of the
lighthouse
keeper, Riino, in
Shadesmar
.
When
Kaladin
visits him (as Riino) at his residence there, the Oracle tells Kaladin that he has come to witness the extraordinary, and that he - the Oracle - shall provide it. Further, that the price is a mere two
marks
of
Stormlight
.
When the man then whips a cloth off a table, he reveals a large
crystalline globe
. It glows with a powerful light, bathing the room in luminescence.
Kaladin blinks at it, wondering whether it's Stormlight.
Seeing Kaladin's hesitancy, the Oracle asks him if he's balking at the price and launches into a  philosophical statement on the spending of money. Kaladin tells the man that he doesn't know what he's talking about.
When the man asks Kaladin to confirm that he came there for a fortune, Kaladin tells him that the future is forbidden.
*
The Oracle then asks Kaladin isn't this why he came to see him, to which Kaladin replies that he's looking for passage because he and his companions had heard that ships come by there.
Notes
*
An oddity about all
Vorin
peoples is that they avoid overtly guessing the future. Even predicting the
highstorms
make many uncomfortable.

PAGE: Riino
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Riino
======================================================================

Riino
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Selish
Nationality
Selish
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Abilities
Worldhopper
Social Information
Aliases
Rii Oracle
Occupation
Lighthouse
Keeper
Appears in
Elantris
,
Oathbringer
Riino
is a member of the Ire
*
who is on
Roshar
because he is just one amongst them who is interested in that world.
In
Shadesmar
, Riino had food and supplies for trade, though they would cost
Kaladin
,
Shallan
,
Adolin
, and
Azure
infused
spheres
.
They seek him out as a supposed fortune-teller who indicates to Kaladin that the very best readings happen at the beginning of a
highstorm
.
Appearance
He is mistaken by Kaladin to be an old
Shin
man, with furrowed, wrinkled skin and a completely bald head.
When he stands up, he proves himself to be rather short. His odd clothing puffs out portions on the arms, the trousers very tight.
Characteristics
Riino can apparently predict highstorms. He checks a small device taken from his pocket and indicates that a coming highstorm is only two minutes off.
Pattern
makes Riino nervous.
History
From outside the lighthouse, Kaladin observes that the old man begs to turn a page in his book, then that he freezes, looking up.
In an accented voice, the old man calls out, wondering just who is outside the lighthouse, and wanting whomever they are to show themselves.
Kaladin sighs, then stands up, resigned to having been found out.
Inside the lighthouse, Riino assumes that Kaladin is there for a fortune, and enters a room as Kaladin follows.
On a small table therein is something lumpish covered by a black cloth, soon revealed to be a
crystalline globe
.
In the midst of Riino telling Kaladin not to touch the globe, that it's only for fortune tellers to do so, Kaladin places his palm upon it and receives a vision.
Upon the vision's passing (though unbeknownst to him), Riino bats Kaladin's hand from the globe, then realizes that Kaladin saw something. That he is
Invested
. He asks Kaladin just what Heightening he is,
then realizes that Kaladin is a
Surgebinder
, and that "It" has begun again.
Shortly thereafter, Riino tells Adolin that a ship will be arriving at any time, and that they should be able to buy passage. Pattern says that this ship is going to
Celebrant
.
Notes
According to Azure, charlatans like Riino aren't uncommon where she comes from (i.e., people who claim to be able to see the future, living off the hopes of others). She tells Kaladin that his society was right to forbid them. Further, that the
spren
do likewise, so Riino's kind have to live in places like Shadesmar, hoping that people will be desperate enough to come to them.
In Elantris, Riino was one of the two people who got into the
Shardpool
on Sel.
The other was
Temoo
(i.e., Galladon).
*
The Ire are Elantrians living in the
Cognitive Realm
who have passed through the Shardpool on Sel.

Soldier
Appears in
Words of Radiance
Rind
is
Highprince
Dalinar
's head quartermaster in his
warcamp
. He is a
lighteyed
Alethi soldier of the tenth
dahn
, but what is known as a "tenner", a lowly rank barely above darkeyes.
He tells
Kaladin
that he has what stock remained of the uniforms for the
Cobalt Guard
, but that he's commissioned some new ones for the members of
Bridge Four
.
He further explains that Dalinar's new members of the Guard are to be outfitted with breastplates, steel caps, and new spears. Plus, chain mail for the battlefield, if they need it.
As he distributes the uniforms
*
to the men, he gives
Shen
a strange look, but delivers the
parshman
a uniform without complaint.
An exasperated Rind explains to the men how to do up the coats, then reacts with horror as
Skar
cuts the patch bearing the insignia of the Cobalt Guard off his shoulder. The others agreed, cutting off the patches, tossing them to the ground.
Kaladin tells Rind that he'll draw up a
glyphpair
insignia for him to use in order to commission new patches. The portly man just sighs as he gathers up the discarded patches.

chasms
of the
Shattered Plains
. Rind is initially confused by the request, but constructs the ladder well.

Notes
*
These are newer uniforms, of a more modern style than Kaladin had worn in his previous military service. Stiff blue trousers and black boots polished to a shine. A buttoned white shirt, only the edges of its collar and cuffs extending beyond the jacket, which came down to the waist and buttoned closed beneath the belt.

PAGE: Rine Hatham
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rine_Hatham
======================================================================

Rine Hatham
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Alethi
Nationality
Alethi
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Social Information
Title(s)
Brightlord
,
Highprince
Family
Wife
(name unknown)
Occupation
Highprince
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Rhythm of War
(mentioned)
Rine Hatham
is a highprince of
Alethkar
. His princedom lies on Alethkar's western border. His wife makes the
Dawncities
her study.
Following the formation of the
Vengeance Pact
, Hatham traveled with his army to the
Shattered Plains
. He belives that "money is behind every war" and, coincidentally, he was well behind in his payments to King
Elhokar
, which was one of the many acts of the highprinces that undermined King Elhokar's rule.
Hatham involved
ardents
in his political maneuvers, an act which was both illegal and a violation of custom ever since the
Hierocracy
. However, the tactic became increasingly popular and was used by many lighteyes.
While three of the highprinces joined
Dalinar
on his expedition to the center of the Shattered Plains,
the greater number had remained behind. When the unexpected
highstorm
came,
Hatham had received word via
spanreed
from scouts out along the Plains. His
warcamp
had been able to get out most of their
spheres
for recharging before the storm hit, giving him a huge amount of
Stormlight
compared to the rest of them. He was becoming a wealthy man as Dalinar traded for infused spheres to work the
Oathgate
and bring in supplies.
Appearance
He is a long-necked, slender man who wears a ruffled green shirt underneath a robelike coat, with a darker green silk scarf around his neck. He also wears a glowing ruby on each of his fingers, the Stormlight from which has been drained away by a
fabrial
made for that purpose.
He wears resplendent
Shardplate
when at the front of his army. He sits upon a
Ryshadium
which is coveted by
Amaram
.
His soldiers wear the gold and red of his army.
Personality
Hatham speaks in a smooth, overly polite way.
Quotes
“
"Religious? ... No, I wouldn't say that. All conflicts are essentially economic in nature. ... Money is behind every war, ... . Religion is but an excuse. Or perhaps a justification."
”
–Hatham to Dalinar
“
"An excuse is what you make after the deed is done, while a justification is what you offer before. ... I would say an excuse is something you claim, but do not believe, Nak-ali. ... While a justification is something you actually believe."
”
–Hatham to Dalinar

======================================================================
PAGE: Rlain
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rlain
======================================================================

Rlain
Rlain
Fan art by Ralf Melevo
[1]
Biographical information
Race
Parsh
Gender
Male
Status
Alive
Abilities
Surgebinding
(
Truthwatcher
)
,
Shardbearer
Social Information
Aliases
Shen
,
Bridger of Minds
Occupation
Spy,
Bridgeman
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Rlain
is a Parshendi amongst the members of
Bridge Four
. He is the only Parshendi who sided with the
Alethi
before the creation of the
Everstorm
. It is revealed that he was a spy for the Parshendi, using
dullform
to disguise himself as a
parshman
. He traveled between
warcamps
for three years before becoming part of Bridge Four.
As Shen, Rlain was placed in Bridge Four as an experiment by
Brightness
Hashal
to see if  parshmen could be trusted to run bridges. The members of
Kaladin
's crew violently distrusted the parshman, wanting to place him up at the front of the bridge to be hit with an arrow instead of a human bridgeman, but Kaladin refused, giving the parshman the name "Shen" and insisting that he was to be treated as a member of the crew.
Rlain was upset when the crew scavenged from dead Parshendi, and even more upset when Kaladin used Parshendi
carapace
as armor during a bridge run to the point where he ended up being left behind on subsequent runs.
He was later retrieved from
Sadeas
's camp and brought with the rest of the bridgemen to
Dalinar
's camp following Sadeas's betrayal and Bridge Four's rescue of Dalinar.
Since Rlain was a spy for about three years in various warcamps, he answers Dalinar's questions of him because he considers Dalinar to be his commanding officer.
Rlain is eventually called back to
Narak
, and gains
warform
there. However, he soon defects from the Parshendi, realizing that the change in
Eshonai
had corrupted his people. Bridge Four accepts him back, and
Sigzil
explains to Kaladin that Dalinar had pardoned Rlain for being a spy, to which Rlain replies that he was a spy for a people who no longer exist. He said this to a different beat, and Kaladin thought he could sense pain in that voice.
Rlain then helps in the Alethi strategy of attacking the
Voidbringers
.
Appearance
After his desertion from Bridge Four as Shen, then his return to Bridge Four as Rlain, he wears a reddish skullplate and a Bridge Four tattoo. His features are sharp and muscular, he has a thick neck and a strong jaw, which is now lined with a red and black beard.
While wearing warform, he is taller and stronger than he'd been before.
On his head he now has carapace armor - distinctively thick and strong, as he holds warform - covering his skull. It stretches out his Bridge Four tattoo, which has transferred to the carapace. He has protrusions on his arms and legs too, which people always want to feel. (They cannot believe they actually grow from his skin, and somehow think it appropriate to try to peek underneath.)
Character
He loves the men of Bridge Four ... and does so because they always try to include him.
He tries not to feel jealous that others like
Colot
and
Lyn
almost seem more a part of the team than he does.
Characteristics
He has the
rhythmic
intonations common to the Parshendi, but he speaks Alethi very well. Better than any parshman Dalinar has heard.
His voice now also has a certain musicality to it - an odd rhythm to his words.
He draws attention from soldiers - outside those of Bridge Four - who consider him to be a danger, regardless of which uniform he wears.
He remains an oddity even to the men of Bridge Four: the parshman they allow to be armed. The potential Voidbringer they had decided to trust.
He and
Skar
were the only two members of Bridge Four who couldn't draw
Stormlight
.
Rlain has always found it to be strange that his fellow bridgemen don't care much about skin color, as among listeners, one's skin patterns at times are a matter of some import.
While he's never held a form of
Odium
, Rlain can hear and feel the difference between
Voidlight
and
anti-Voidlight
.
Beliefs
Rlain believes that one of his kind has to read human emotion in each their expressions and the way they move, not in their voices. He suspects that maybe this is why emotion
spren
come so often to humans, more often than to
listeners
. Without the rhythms, men need help understanding one another.
He also believes that it is sad that humans are so burdened by always being in
mateform
. That they are always distracted by the emotions and passions of mating, and have not yet reached a place where they can put that aside. He feels embarrassed for them because they are simply too concerned about what a person should and shouldn't be doing.
Biography
During his years spent as a spy, he'd adopted dullform, which heard the rhythms weakly. It had been hard for him to be apart from them.
They weren't quite true
songs
; they were beats with hints of tonality and harmony. He could attune one of several dozen to match his mood, or - conversely - to help alter his mood.
According to Rlain, his people had always assumed that humans were deaf to the rhythms, but he wasn't convinced. Perhaps it was his imagination, but it seemed that sometimes they responded to certain rhythms.
It comforted him to think that they might someday learn to hear the rhythms. Perhaps then he wouldn't feel so alone.
Rlain's people are now gone. Though parshmen have awakened, they are not listeners. As far as he knows, he is the last.
When
Teft
leads others in a streaking wave of light of
Windrunners
overhead, Rlain looks up, and finds himself attuning Longing before stomping it out. He attunes Peace instead.
When both
Eth
and
Yake
ask him about the
Fused
, he tells them that his people did everything they could to separate themselves from those creatures. That they went into hiding long ago, and swore they would never accept
forms of power
again.
Further, that he didn't know what changed. That his people must have been tricked somehow. That the Fused are as much his enemies as they are theirs ... and more, even. He cannot say what they will do, but does say that he spent he spent his entire life trying to avoid thinking of them.
When
Rock
asks the bridgemen who came to him stating that they no longer felt they fit in with Bridge Four, and when some reply that they think they're alone, feel like they're outsiders, yet they don't ask how he feels, Rlain wonders whether it's childish of him to feel frustrated. He questions whether they know what it's like to be of an entirely different species. A species with which they're currently at war ... a species whose people had all been either murdered or corrupted.
Rlain sees that people in the tower of
Urithiru
watch him with outright hatred. Though his friends don't, he sees that they still like to pat themselves on the back for that fact.
When Kaladin then tells him that he knows how he feels, Rlain asks him if he really, actually does. Then, when Kaladin asks Rlain if he can tell him, Rlain says that he can try.
Rlain's time in
mateform
was not what he anticipated. While it was not a disaster, it turned out that he was solely attracted to other men.
Relationships
Kaladin - Rlain believes that Kaladin is a good man. That for all his faults, he tries even more than the rest of them.
Renarin
- The quiet,
lighteyed
man usually made a point of speaking with Rlain.
There is definitely chemistry between them, and they are now in a same-sex relationship with one another.
Family
Bridge Four is his family, now that those from Narak are gone. Eshonai,
Varanis
,
Thude
, etc..
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Rock
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rock
======================================================================

Rock
Rock
Fan art by Elizabeth Kunkle
[1]
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Unkalaki
Nationality
Unkalaki
Status
Unknown
Abilities
Sighted
Physical Description
Hair Color
Red
Eye Color
Soft Brown
Social Information
Aliases
Numuhukumakiaki'aialunamor
,
Lunamor
Title(s)
Horneater,
Bridge Four
Lieutenant,
Bridge Four Quartermaster
Family
Rock's Family
Occupation
Bridge Four cook,
Squire
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Rock
was a
bridgeman
in
Sadeas
's army. He became Bridge Four's cook and barber. He is an Unkalaki, or Horneater, whose full name is Numuhukumakiaki'aialunamor.
This name is a description of a type of rock his father found shortly before he was born. The name is hard to pronounce, which is why people simply call him "Rock". He is called Lunamor amongst his family members.
He is married and has sent letters to his
family
via
spanreed
to the
Horneater Peaks
message station, paid for by his first saved wages, though the Peaks are so far away that news of them has not yet returned.
That is, not until he and his fellows follow a plume of smoke on the
Shattered Plains
and find a ransacked and raided caravan there. Rock is overjoyed to find that his family is there too.
Appearance
Rock
Fan art by Nat Rodgers
[2]
Rock is a hefty man with thick limbs;
a beefy, tan-skinned man,
*
with an oval face. He has deep red hair,
and long red-blond sideburns which cover his cheeks and curve down to his chin. His chin, from beneath his lips, is shaved clean. (He takes great pride in his facial hair.) Rock shaves the hair upon his head up the center, from directly above his eyebrows back.
He is also nearly seven feet tall,
and a broad man; he's nearly twice as broad as
Kaladin
and a few inches taller than Kaladin too.
Personality
Rock is inviting, engaging and even playful. His outlook remains positive despite the obstacles Bridge Four faces. He is a very loyal and caring man with great faith in Kaladin.
He is known to be sarcastic and humorous in his interactions with others. He cares for all members of Bridge Four as he would his family, which is shown with his interactions with
Shen
.
He is also very proud of his cooking. He refuses to allow poor food to be cooked for members of Bridge Four. A testament to this is evidenced when Rock goes with
Dalinar
's army as he wouldn't leave his friend (i.e., Kaladin) to the mercy of the camp cooks.
[citation needed]
Character
Rock does not like kicking complainers because it hurts his toes. But, he says that big Unkalaki feet like his were made for it.
Rock had always been taught that the first rule of warfare was to know one's enemy, though his "enemies" now on the
Shattered Plains
are his friends. They come to each meal expecting greatness, and he fights to prove himself time and time again. He wages war with breads and soups, sating appetites and satisfying stomachs.
He has become fond of the barren, windswept flatland of the Shattered Plains and is surprised to discover that he had missed it.
Characteristics
According to
Brandon
, Rock has not always been a third son.
Beliefs
Rock thinks that ale should be a drink for women, not for large Horneater men.
Rock believes that everyone else thinks him to be a loud, insufferable lout ... so for him to be something else would not be bad thing. (He doesn't worry that he isn't the person everyone else thinks he is.)
Further, he believes that men do well learning many different skills ... but that they also do well using what the gods have given them.
According to Rock, any chef worth his spoons learns to talk, because cooking is an art - and art is subjective. One man could love an ice sculpture while another thought it boring. It's the same with food and drink. It does not make the food broken, or the person broken, to not be liked.
Abilities
Rock is able to see spren whether they choose to be seen by him. He apologizes to the spren in the chasm where he and members of Bridge Four are practicing with Kaladin's abilities, naming those spren,
mafah'liki
.
He claims that he is
alaii'iku
... that he can see spren while others cannot. He believes this is due to the others' status as
airsick lowlanders
; that the air below the Horneater Peaks makes their brains stop working correctly.
He is one of the few people, so far, who can see Kaladin's
honorspren
,
Syl
,
as he was born with this ability.
Rock the Pacifist
Fan art by sheep
[3]
Rock is an accomplished cook,
**
barber and archer. His role in Bridge Four is as someone whom Kaladin and the entire bridgecrew rely upon to feed them each night.
Rock believes that
chull
head is very good to eat; that it is the best part of a chull.
As a barber, Rock's hand is smooth and careful. He shaves the beards of any amongst the men who want it with a deft touch. Because of Rock's gift, the men have neatly-trimmed beards or clean-shaven faces ... even bald heads.
Despite his skill with archery, he refuses to fight,
***
and claims that they don't fight among the Unkalaki, and instead have drinking competitions to settle disputes.
Rock is determined by Kaladin to have had training as a wrestler.
History
Rock is a third son of the Unkalaki people, and therefore one who cannot be wasted in battle. Amongst the Unkalaki, the fourth son or below - the
tuanalikina
- is fit to be a soldier.
He'd always been a cook, since he could toddle up a step stool to a counter and stick his fingers in sticky dough nearby where his mother hummed and gave him her careful instructions.
Still, he'd once trained with a bow. But soldiers needed to eat, and
nuatoma
guards each did several jobs, even guards with his particular heritage and blessings.
So, he followed his nuatoma (i.e.,
Kef'ha
) to the Shattered Plains to kill a
Shardbearer
so his nuatoma (hence, the Unkalaki people as a whole), could have a set of
Shardplate
. His nuatoma decided to challenge
Highprince
Sadeas, by whom he was defeated, thereby leaving the remaining Unkalaki in Sadeas's service.
Rock was originally used as Sadeas's cook, but was promptly sent to the bridgecrews after Rock decided to use chull dung to cook an array of dishes for Sadeas's pleasure.
“
"Wait,"
Teft said.
"You put chull dung in Highprince Sadeas's soup?" "Er, yes,"
Rock said.
"Actually, I put this thing in his bread too. And used it as a garnish on the pork steak. And made a chutney out of it for the buttered garams."
”
–Rock, in response to
Teft
Later, he tells a story about
Tana'kai
in a
tavern
on the Shattered Plains to members of Bridge Four, then elaborates about the hot springs on top of his homeland:
“
"On top, is water. Beneath, is not. Is something else. Water of life. The place of the gods. This thing is true. I have met a god myself."
”
Bridge Four
The Way of Kings
Rock immediately fell into obedience with Kaladin and accepted him as their bridgeleader, because when it was his turn at the front (which means sure death),
Kaladin took Rock's place and gave Rock the relatively safe position at the back.
Words of Radiance
Kaladin later names him a lieutenant and quartermaster for the food for the thousands.
Despite that he doesn't have a
slave
brand to cover up, Rock insists that he will get the Bridge Four
glyphs
tattooed into his forehead, as did some of his his brethren.
Kaladin tells Rock that he needs him to train cooks for the other barracks because he wants them to be self-sufficient.
Oathbringer
Rock
Fan art by lilikaia
[4]
Since he looks similar to a man whom
Shallan
had stabbed, Rock is stabbed through the hand by the
Unmade
,
Re-Shephir
, who'd impersonated
Veil
in a long coat and a hat.
As the non-combatant cook of Bridge Four, who creates stews for them, he is often accompanied by
Huio
, whom he has told to add
lazbo
to the other bins of
shiki
, as Huio himself had initially presumed to do to one bin.
He is also often assisted by
Hobber
, and is happy to have him because the man is earnest and helpful. Similarly,
Renarin
is often close by and communicative.
When Rock asks
Skar
to show him what he's learned with regard to Stormlight, and - after Skar's instruction - he actually manages to draw some in himself, and is delighted and surprised.
Assembling on a plateau to his left, Rock then sees strong gods, their focus the practicing bridgemen and hopefuls.
Soon thereafter, Rock's family is found on the Plains. After greeting them all, when his
wife
asks him what happened to him, he wonders just how he can explain that the man she'd always said was so strong had wished to die. Had been a coward, had given up, near the end.
Much later, after
Amaram
had ingested a
gem
that held the Unmade,
Yelig-nar
, Rock killed him with a
Shardbow
, impaling Amaram twice; once in the mouth and a second time in his '
gemheart
'.
Rock considers what he'd done to be an oath broken, despite it having saved Kaladin's life.
Rhythm of War
Feeling guilty for having broken his oath of non-violence, Rock travels back to his homeland to face judgement. Kaladin reluctantly agrees, sending Skar and
Drehy
as escorts.
Later, Kaladin reunites with Skar and Drehy and they report that they will never be seeing Rock again, implying that Rock's judgement was passed and his execution carried out.
Notes
Near the end of Words of Radiance, he calls Shallan cousin, due to their similar hair color.
*
Brandon
envisions Rock's skin as appearing like that of a Native American.
**
According to Brandon, Rock's favorite ingredient in his stews is chili powder (i.e., his version of a chili powder). Rock would make his stews super spicy.
***
Rock, nevertheless, kills Amaram with a
Grandbow
.
Trivia
According to Brandon, readers can find Rock in the 1998/99 version of
Dragonsteel
. He's largely the same character with the same name - though this was before he and Bridge Four were moved to The Stormlight Archive.

Roion
Unstylized version of Roion's Glyph
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Alethi
Nationality
Alethi
Gender
Male
Status
Dead
Social Information
Title(s)
Brightlord
,
Highprince
Occupation
Highprince
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
Roion
was a highprince of
Alethkar
. He had great archers, superior to those of the other highprinces.
However, he won the fewest
gemhearts
of all of the highprinces.
Roion was one of only three highprinces to, quite unexpectedly, accompany
Dalinar
to the center of the
Shattered Plains
to assault the
Parshendi
and locate the
Oathgate
.
When Dalinar was attacked by
Szeth
near the center of the Shattered Plains, Roion attempted to defend him and was
lashed
to a point in the sky by Szeth. He later plummeted from the sky and and hit the ground with a sickening crunch.
Roion was pronounced dead by Dalinar, stating that he fell with honor.
Personality
Roion is described as cowardly by many and has been compared to a turtle on more than one occasion. However at the battle at the end of Words of Radiance, Dalinar says that Roion is not cowardly but careful - and in his defence of Dalinar he showed real bravery.
[citation needed]
Roion is not a key character in the book, so doesn't really have any personality traits other than cowardice until his surprising alliance with Dalinar towards the end of his life. Otherwise he is presumably a lot like the other highprinces in his greed and squabbles as he often gets lumped together with them when Dalinar mentions them.
[citation needed]

PAGE: Rushu
CATEGORIES: Characters

Rushu
Rushu
Fan art by Treefin
[1]
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Alethi
Nationality
Alethi
Gender
Female
Status
Alive
Physical Description
Hair Color
Shaved
Occupation
Ardent
Appears in
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Rhythm of War
Rushu
is an ardent and one of the
Kholin
scholars. She is critical of the
glyphs
-
Sixty-two days. Death follows
- that have been etched upon the the wall of
Dalinar
's room in the palace on the
Shattered Plains
.
“
Look at the sloppy lines! The improper symmetry. Whoever did this is
not
practiced with drawing glyphs.
*
”
She attempts to intercept
Navani
with a report on the glyphs on their way to the test of a
fabrial
to protect Alethi soldiers within a circular wooden tower, the platform of which is seemingly unsupported in the air, but she is rebuffed.
Rushu asked why a
Shardblade
can be summoned and dismissed even if its
gemstone
has gone dun.
Given her concern,
artifabrians
are back to knowing absolutely nothing about how Shardblades were crafted. It seems the gemstone's purpose is
only
used in initially bonding the Blade - something the
Knights Radiant
didn't need to do.
Appearance
Rushu is young, with long, lush eyelashes, button-like lips and delicate features.
Personality
Rushu questions why the
Almighty
gave beards to men but not to women. Further, she questions why, in
Vorin
society, that it is considered feminine for women to have long hair, thinking it should be a more masculine trait.
Characteristics
She was the first ardent to ever write a
ketek
poem backwards.
[citation needed]
Rushu is one of several ardents (along with
Kadash
and
Ladent
) to reject uniting Dalinar and Navani in marriage.
According to Navani, Rushu pays attention to the oddest things, but never seems to notice important details.
History
Due to her attractive appearance, Rushu joined the
ardentia
to escape the advances of men. Even so, male ardents always want to work with her. Fortunately, she is brilliant.
Navani confirms that Rushu is good at what she does, but that she's a little oblivious sometimes. Conversely, she states that Rushu knows handwriting better than anyone, indicating that it's one of Rushu's many areas of interest.
Notes
*
She identifies
Renarin
's symmetry as improper.

Ruthar

Title(s)
Brightlord
,
Highprince
Family
Jayla
(wife),
Relis
(son),
Ivanar
(son),
Elit
(nephew)
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
(mentioned),
Rhythm of War
Ruthar
is a Highprince of Alethkar.
His princedom is bordered to the north by that of Highprince
Vamah
, to the east by that of Highprince
Dalinar
, and to the south by the
Sunmaker
Mountains
.
As a member of
Gavilar
's army of twenty-nine years ago, in a battle against
Kalanor
's army, Ruthar brought down
Gallam
, winning both
Plate
and
Blade
for his son.
Personality
He is a brutal and cruel man who regularly beats his children.
History
During the war in
Emul
, Ruthar arrives there with
Dalinar
but is not allowed to be engaged in battle panning. He is indignant to see
Jasnah
giving
Mink
advice regarding battle planning because traditionally, war was men's art. Provoked by
Wit
, he engages in battle with Jasnah and she stabbes him with Wit's sword.
Renarin
heals him. Jasnah forfeits him of his title, passing it to his elder son.

PAGE: Rysn Ftori
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Rysn_Ftori
======================================================================

Rysn Ftori
Rysn Ftori
Fan art by paintweaver
[1]
Biographical information
Ethnicity
Thaylen
Nationality
Thaylen
Gender
Female
Status
Alive
Occupation
Merchant, Clerk, Ship Captain
Appears in
The Way of Kings
,
Words of Radiance
,
Oathbringer
,
Dawnshard
Rysn Ftori
is a Thaylen apprentice merchant to
Vstim
, whom she finds to be a very strange but knowledgeable person, and from whom she can learn a lot about mercantile enterprise. She is a practitioner of
Vorinism
to keep up appearances, but she does not particularly take some of the traditions to heart; she finds covering her
safehand
merely a silly tradition. She is also very observant, as evidenced by her listening to a sales pitch between her master and a
Shin
farmer called
Thresh-son-Esan
. From it, she deduces that the Shin haggle to undermine their products, rather than boasting about them.
Rysn
Fan art by botanicaxu
[2]
She finds Shin culture to be very odd, and because of this her master, Vstim, tries to get her to appreciate their culture by ordering her to cut out a section of grass from
Shinovar
, which didn't move like she was used to, and care for it until she no longer finds it strange. She later names it
Tyvnk
, which means Sullen.
The next year, Rysn and her babsk were in the
Reshi Isles
trading with
Talik
, an occupant of a
Tai-na
called
Relu-na
. Vstim feigned sickness in an attempt to give Rysn practice trading with real stakes. When Rysn thinks the trade is at stake, she acts rashly and attempts to speak to Relu-na itself by lowering herself down near its head. However, the rope she was clinging to snapped and she fell a large distance. She only survived because the island's soul itself saved her by cushioning the fall. In the end, Vstim gets the corpse of a
larkin
, his original trade which was never at stake, but Rysn is given a very rare live larkin by the island itself.
Rysn now keeps
Queen Fen
's ledgers. She believes the job to be a good one, with an office in the Thaylen
Gemstone Reserve
. Vstim had traded favors for her to get the position. Her apprenticeship with him is now finished, and she is a free woman.
However, due to her fall in the Reshi Isles, she now cannot feel her legs, nor can she control certain bodily functions. She has to rely on her porters to move her, though she can balance while sitting.
Her duties include annotating the queen's pending mercantile contracts with references to previous ones, keeping the queen's personal vault in the Gemstone Reserve, preparing weekly expenditure reports, and accounting the queen's salary as a portion of taxable income from various Thaylen interests at home and abroad.
During this time, Vstim accepted a position that allowed him to become her direct superior. He bought her a ship of her own and offered her the chance to explore the world as a merchant once again, something Rysn thought wouldn't be possible. During the inspection, one of the guards - a
Parshendi
spy - killed the other guard in the vault and wounded Vstim. Rysn was able to drag herself away and use a hastily constructed contraption combining a crossbow and her own wheelchair to kill the spy. She was able to alert the guards who attempted to convey her and Vstim out of the city with the gemheart the spy was after. However, their transport was attacked and the gem taken, leaving Rysn and Vstim's fates ultimately unknown in the wake of the battle. Their actions however did alert
Dalinar
to the fact the enemy wanted the gemheart in the first place.
Rysn tells Vstim about the Sleepless, but is prohibited from telling of them to anyone else, even Queen Fen.
Characteristics
Rysn
Fan art by lamaery
[3]
Apparently,
Renarin
(referred to only as " ... that
Radiant
" by Vstim) cannot heal Rysn's legs. She states that it's been too long since her accident, which she deems appropriate, that this is her consequence - payment for the contract she entered into willingly the moment she climbed down the side of the
greatshell
.
According to
Brandon
, Rysn did not use her
Dawnshard
via
Command
in that story, and indeed is incapable of it.
Rysn is forbidden from
bonding
a
spren
.
Beliefs
Rysn believes that one must trade from a position of power, something that she can never do again as a cripple.
“
Her babsk taught a different way of seeing the world, training her to ask,
"What need can I fulfill?”
That was the true purpose of a merchant. To find complementary needs, then bridge the distance between them so everyone benefited.
[citation needed]
”
Q&A with Brandon
Q. With
savants
, the more they use the magic (the more
Kaza
Soulcasts
, the more
Spook
burns
tin
), the more pronounced the effect. Is there something similar going on with the Dawnshards? Would Rysn change more and more as time goes by? Savants do,
Vessels
do ...
Q. Yes, she would change more as time goes by.

PAGE: Sah
CATEGORIES: Characters
URL: https://stormlightarchive.fandom.com/wiki/Sah
======================================================================

Sah
Biographical information
Race
Parsh
Gender
Male
Status
Dead
Family
Vai
(daughter)
Occupation
Singer
Appears in
Oathbringer
Sah
was a singer who had previously been trapped in
slaveform
. He described it as "living in a fog ... knowing deep in your soul that something is profoundly wrong ... [but] not being able to say a single word to stop it."
He had a mate who was sold to become a breeder because she bore him a healthy daughter.
Appearance
Sah had marbled skin - red on white - like most singers from
Alethkar
. He usually wore
workform
with both hair strands and dark red beard strands.
Personality
He was a contemplative individual, but also one quite angered with humanity for keeping him and his people enslaved, as well as afraid that humanity would once again take away his newfound freedom.
Biography
Sah escaped with his daughter from a village near
Hearthstone
when first the
Everstorm
passed.  He was freed from slaveform when his
Connection
and
Identity
were restored soon thereafter. He and his daughter then joined the group led by
Khen
and the
Voidspren
Yixli
.
With Xixli's guidance, Sah traveled to
Revolar
with Khen's group,
and "captured"
Kaladin
along the way.
Though Kaladin taught the group how to survive on the run,
once he'd left, the
Fused
accused the group of harboring a spy and enslaved them with captive humans,
putting them to work hauling lumber.
Their treatment angered Sah, as did the removal of his daughter, but he didn't know what to do about it.
Later, Sah and the group were defended by a captive
Moash
, who taught them how to fight, because they were going to be amongst the first of forces to attack
Kholinar
as just a part of their punishment.
When Sah and the group assaulted the
palace
, he and Kaladin recognized one another. Even so, Sah killed
Beard
in the melee, a member of the
Wall Guard
, and was then killed in turn by
Noromin
, which was witnessed by Khen.

Sakin
is the wife of the lighthouse keeper, Puuli. She had thought herself quite high and blessed when she'd landed her new husband. She'd moved into the man's fine stone hut in a prime spot for growing a garden behind the northern cliffs of the town.
She weeps as Puuli tells her that the
storm
that had passed was truly tragic, even as he is secretly happy about it. She weeps for her husband.

Sani
is a young woman from
Hearthstone
who needed surgery on her
freehand
.
“
Sani had three broken fingers and the skin on her hand was scraped and gouged, the wound cluttered with  sticks and dirt.  The third finger was the worst, shattered and twisted nastily, splinters of bone protruding through the skin.
